briefs.title: Council to vote on opposing BC Housing complex care project at 5389 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will consider a motion to formally refuse support for a BC Housing supportive and complex care housing development at 5389 Imperial Street, following strong community opposition to the proposed site.
briefs.action_required: Attend the February 10 Council meeting to follow the vote if this project affects your area.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — BC Housing Imperial Street motion

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Consider Imperial Oil Petition — Details Redacted
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider a petition (a formal written request) submitted by Imperial Oil, covering agenda pages 221–302. The full contents are redacted, so the specific ask or proposal is not publicly known at this time.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision and check for an unredacted version to understand how this may affect your business.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Petition Opposing Supportive Housing Project at 5389 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Council will consider a supplementary petition from Burnaby Neighbourhood Watch — signed by 981 residents — opposing a proposed BC Housing supportive/complex care housing development at 5389 Imperial Street. This follows an earlier petition with 11,749 signatures submitted in January 2026.
briefs.action_required: Attend the February 10, 2026 Council meeting if you want to follow this housing decision.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — supplementary petition considered

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Healthcare

briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby's five-year financial plan covering 2026 to 2030
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would adopt the city's five-year financial plan for 2026–2030, outlining how the city plans to raise and spend money each year. The plan projects total revenues rising from roughly $873M in 2026 to over $1.05B by 2030, with property taxes remaining the largest funding source at 44.8% of 2026 revenue.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — rising property taxes could increase your business operating costs.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2026–2030 Financial Plan Bylaw — council adoption vote

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Retail

briefs.title: Council to Consider Update on Burnaby's Online Recreation Registration System Improvements
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to receive an information update on improvements made to the city's online recreation registration system (WebReg), including new pass options, fairer booking rules, and better online self-service for residents.
briefs.action_required: If you use Burnaby recreation facilities, review the new pass options and updated registration rules at the city's website.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby's 10 Park Development Projects Planned for 2026–2027
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to receive a report outlining 10 park improvement projects planned for 2026–2027, including playground replacements, a new basketball court, spray park design, and trail upgrades — all funded through the City's 2026–2030 capital plan.
briefs.action_required: Check the City of Burnaby's project webpage and social media for updates and feedback surveys on parks near your business.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan — Community Input Report
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review a draft 'What We Heard' report from summer 2025 community engagement, which will shape a proposed Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan guiding future investment in parks, pools, arenas, and cultural facilities across the city.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the release of the draft Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan in early 2026 to review and comment.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Making Life Better PRC Plan — draft directions released for public review

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Parks, Recreation & Culture Plan Moves to Drafting Phase
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to receive an update on the city's long-range Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan, which is now entering Phase 3 — the drafting stage. A second round of public engagement is planned for March 2026, giving residents a chance to review and comment on the draft plan before it is finalized.
briefs.action_required: Participate in the public engagement sessions planned for March 2026 to shape Burnaby's parks and recreation priorities.

key_dates.label: Burnaby PRC Plan Phase 4 public engagement opens — comment period

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Building Permit Activity Report — December 2025
briefs.summary: Council is set to review Burnaby's Building Permit Activity Report No. 12 for 2025, which tracks construction activity across the city. The report shows 1,225 permits issued year-to-date, representing over $3.15 billion in construction value — up significantly from 2024.
briefs.action_required: Review permit volume trends if you plan to apply for a building, renovation, or trade permit in Burnaby soon.

briefs.title: Council to receive Burnaby building permit activity report for December 2025
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a routine information report covering all building permits issued in Burnaby during December 2025. No new rules or approvals are being voted on — this is a monthly update on local construction activity.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $10,000 for staff to attend 2026 BCRPA Symposium in Vancouver
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on spending up to $10,000 to send up to six Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee members to the 2026 BCRPA Symposium in Vancouver, running April 28–May 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: 2026 BCRPA Symposium takes place — Vancouver

briefs.title: Council to consider renewing road marking paint and glass bead supply contract
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a second renewal of the contract for road marking paint and glass beads — materials used to paint lines and markings on city roads. The proposed 24-month renewal would run from March 1, 2026 to February 29, 2028, at an estimated cost of $380,000 with no price increase per unit.
briefs.action_required: If you supply road marking materials, monitor the council decision — this contract renewal may affect your bid opportunities.

key_dates.label: Burnaby road marking paint contract 2nd renewal — proposed start date

briefs.title: Council to vote on $380,000 contract renewal for road marking paint and glass beads
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on renewing a supply contract with Cloverdale Paint Inc. for road marking paint and glass beads, valued at an estimated $380,000 (taxes included) over two years.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the February 10, 2026 council decision if you supply road marking materials in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — road marking paint contract renewal vote

briefs.title: Council to Consider Renewing Fence Rental Contract for 2026–2027 at $267,000
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a fence rental contract for one more year (June 2026 to May 2027) at a proposed cost of $267,000 — a 6.8% increase over the current year. If approved, the total value of this contract since 2022 would reach an estimated $766,000.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if you supply fencing or compete for City rental contracts.

key_dates.label: Burnaby fence rental contract renewal proposed start date
Burnaby fence rental contract renewal proposed end date

briefs.title: Council to vote on $267,000 fence rental contract renewal with Super Save
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on renewing a temporary fence rental contract with Super Save Fence Rentals Inc. for $267,000 (taxes included), covering June 1, 2026 to May 31, 2027. The fencing supports city events, construction sites, and public safety needs.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on February 10, 2026 — it may affect fence rental supply in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby fence rental contract renewal period begins — Super Save Fence Rentals
Burnaby fence rental contract renewal period ends — Super Save Fence Rentals

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on $7.2M extension of Burnaby's police dispatch contract with E-COMM
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on extending its police dispatch contract with E-COMM Emergency Communications For BC by one year (Jan–Dec 2025) at an estimated cost of $7,194,789, while a provincial review of E-COMM is completed and a new long-term agreement is drafted.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this affects how Burnaby's emergency dispatch is funded and operated in 2025.

key_dates.label: E-COMM police dispatch contract extension period begins — Burnaby
E-COMM police dispatch contract extension period ends — Burnaby

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Proposed $7.3M renewal of Burnaby's E-COMM 911 dispatch contract
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing its contract with E-COMM, the region's 911 emergency dispatch service, for 2026 at a cost of $7.3 million — a roughly 1% increase over 2025. This would bring the total spent on this contract since 2013 to over $55 million.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this affects public safety services that all Burnaby businesses rely on.

key_dates.label: E-COMM 911 dispatch Contract Renewal #5 proposed start date

briefs.title: Council to vote on $7.3M renewal of Burnaby police dispatch contract with E-COMM
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing its police dispatch contract with E-COMM Emergency Communications for one year (2026), at an estimated cost of $7,300,000 — about $500,000 less than earlier estimates due to lower expected call volumes and cost savings.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on February 10, 2026 — this affects public safety services in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: E-COMM police dispatch contract renewal period begins — Burnaby
E-COMM police dispatch contract renewal period ends — Burnaby

briefs.title: Council to Consider Approving Expanded Hazardous Material Survey Contract Worth $558,800
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a pending contract change that would add $240,000 to an existing hazardous material survey contract, bringing the total estimated value to $558,800 over five years. The extra funds would cover survey work for the City's Civic Projects division, which was added to the contract's scope.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this contract affects firms providing hazardous material or environmental survey services to the City.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $240,000 contract increase for hazardous building material surveys
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on adding $240,000 (including tax) to an existing contract with Astech Consultants Ltd. to carry out pre-demolition hazardous building material surveys — inspections required before tearing down buildings — at various City-owned sites.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision on February 10, 2026 — this may affect demand for environmental consulting or demolition work.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — hazardous building material survey contract change

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Optional $193,000 Increase to BAH Program Contract
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on an optional contract change that would add $193,000 — a 3.8% increase — to the existing BAH Program service contract, bringing the total estimated contract value to $807,000 over four years.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on February 10, 2026 — this contract change could affect related service providers.

key_dates.label: Burnaby BAH Program optional contract change — council vote
Burnaby BAH Program Contract Renewal #3 term ends

briefs.title: Council to vote on $193,000 contract extension for seniors housekeeping program
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on extending a contract with Comfort Keepers to continue light housekeeping services for seniors under the Better at Home Program through December 31, 2026. The $193,000 extension is fully funded by a BC government grant through United Way and fees paid by participating seniors — no city tax dollars are involved.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if you provide home support or senior care services in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Better at Home housekeeping contract extension end date — Burnaby

briefs.title: Council to Consider: $700,000 Increase to Archaeological Consulting Contract
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a pending contract change that would add $700,000 to an existing archaeological consulting services agreement, bringing the total estimated contract value to approximately $2.04 million over three years.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect firms bidding on or working near Burnaby city contracts.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $700,000 contract extension for archaeological consulting services
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on adding $700,000 to an existing contract with Campfire Archaeology and Heritage Ltd. to continue providing archaeological consulting — expert reviews of land and heritage sites — for City construction projects through 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision on February 10, 2026 — this affects firms bidding on City construction contracts.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — archaeological consulting contract change approval

briefs.title: Council to Consider Adding 3 Toyota Tacoma Trucks to City Fleet
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a contract change that would add 3 more Toyota Tacoma pickup trucks to the city fleet for $174,384, using the same pricing as the original 8-truck purchase approved in 2025.

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Buying 3 more Toyota Tacoma trucks for $174,384
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on adding three Toyota Tacoma pickup trucks to a existing contract with OpenRoad Toyota Richmond, at an estimated cost of $174,384 including taxes. The trucks would replace three aging city vehicles used by the Engineering department.

briefs.title: Council to vote on: $20M fuel supply contract with 4Refuel Canada LP
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a five-year, $20-million contract to 4Refuel Canada LP to supply gasoline, diesel, and biodiesel to City facilities starting March 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on February 10 — this contract affects fuel suppliers and logistics companies bidding on City work.

key_dates.label: Burnaby fuel supply contract with 4Refuel Canada LP takes effect
Burnaby fuel supply contract with 4Refuel Canada LP expires

briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Natural Resources, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to vote on $648,206 garbage truck purchase for Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract worth $648,206 (including taxes) to Rollins Machinery Ltd. for a new tandem axle front-load refuse truck to replace an aging city vehicle.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $621,838 contract for two new Burnaby garbage trucks
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract worth $621,838 (including taxes) to Rollins Machinery Ltd. for two new split body refuse trucks — specialized garbage trucks that can carry two types of waste at once — to replace two aging city vehicles.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Lochdale School Park Playground Replacement Planned for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review site and context maps for a proposed playground replacement at Lochdale School Park, with the upgrade planned for 2026. The maps show the current playground equipment — some dating back to 1989 — and the site's relationship to nearby parks and schools.

key_dates.label: Lochdale School Park playground replacement — planned completion year

briefs.title: Council to Consider Permanently Closing Lochdale School Park Junior Playground
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on permanently decommissioning the junior playground at Lochdale School Park and deferring its replacement until future budget cycles can fund it. The playground was closed in December 2025 after failing to meet safety standards, and no replacement is currently budgeted.
briefs.action_required: If you use or operate near Lochdale School Park, watch the February 10 council decision for updates.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — Lochdale playground decision vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Burnaby Procedure Bylaw update for advisory body oversight
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on amendments to the Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024 that would clarify how the rules (called Terms of Reference) for City advisory bodies are created and updated — requiring review by the Executive Committee before going to Council for approval.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public notice posted on Burnaby's website and eNewsletter to see the proposed changes.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Procedure Bylaw amendment — Council vote meeting date

briefs.title: Council to Consider Restaurant Use at 8340 Fraser Reach Court, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider approving a restaurant use at 8340 Fraser Reach Court — a space of about 2,217 sq ft (206 m²) near Wiggins St and Riverbend Dr. No decision has been made yet.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — it could affect nearby food service businesses or your own expansion plans.
briefs.industries: Food Service, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Consider Removing Restaurant-Only Restriction at Burnaby Industrial Site
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on removing a legal restriction (called a covenant) that currently requires a 2,217 sq. ft. space at 8340 Fraser Reach Court to be used only as a restaurant. If approved, the space could be converted to light industrial use.
briefs.action_required: If you are interested in leasing this space as a restaurant, contact Oxford Properties before the February 10, 2026 council meeting.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — covenant discharge at 8340 Fraser Reach Court

briefs.industries: Food Service, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Amendments to Add Five New Zoning Districts
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on sweeping changes to Burnaby's Zoning Bylaw that would create five new zoning districts — including Employment (E1), Industrial (I1), Parks (PM1), Institutional (PM2), and Agricultural (AM1) — and update dozens of land-use definitions and rules to align with the city's OCP 2050 long-term plan.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property or business location falls under a new or changed zoning district — it could affect what you're allowed to do there.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Agriculture, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on new zoning rules for industrial, parks, and farm land
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed zoning bylaw that would set out what businesses and uses are allowed — and under what conditions — on lands zoned for employment, industrial, parks, institutional, and agricultural purposes across the city.
briefs.action_required: Check which zone your property falls under and review the permitted uses list before the council vote.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on five new non-residential zoning districts
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on proposed changes to Burnaby's Zoning Bylaw that would create five new zoning districts — areas with specific rules about what kinds of businesses and uses are allowed — to carry out the city's long-term land use plan, Burnaby 2050.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — new zoning rules could affect where and how your business can operate.

briefs.title: Council to Hear Update on Second Narrows Dredging Works from Port Authority
briefs.summary: The Vancouver Fraser Port Authority is set to appear before Burnaby City Council on February 10, 2026, to give an update on dredging works at Second Narrows. Dredging means removing sediment from the waterway to keep it deep enough for ships to pass safely.
briefs.action_required: Attend the February 10, 2026 Council meeting if the Second Narrows dredging works affect your business.

key_dates.label: Vancouver Fraser Port Authority Second Narrows dredging update — Burnaby Council presentation

briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Natural Resources, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approves 2026–2030 Financial Plan and multiple city contracts
briefs.summary: At its January 27, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on a range of items including a five-year financial plan, housing agreement changes, and several construction and service contracts totalling millions of dollars.
briefs.action_required: Review the approved contracts and financial plan to see if any city spending affects your business or industry.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Rewrite to Reshape Employment, Industrial & Agricultural Rules
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a major rewrite of its Zoning Bylaw — the rulebook that controls what businesses can operate where — that would cut zoning categories from 53 to 15 and introduce new rules for employment, industrial, and agricultural lands, including restrictions on self-storage facilities near SkyTrain stations.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed new zoning districts at the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Rewrite website to see how your business or property may be affected.

briefs.title: Council to vote on opposing BC Housing complex care project at 5389 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will consider a motion to formally refuse support for a BC Housing supportive and complex care housing development at 5389 Imperial Street, following strong community opposition to the proposed site.
briefs.action_required: Attend the February 10 Council meeting to follow the vote if this project affects your area.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — BC Housing Imperial Street motion

briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider a petition (a formal written request) related to a redacted Imperial lands matter, covering agenda pages 221–302. The full details are not publicly available, but Council will review and vote on the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — this could affect nearby property owners or development plans.

briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would adopt the city's five-year financial plan for 2026–2030, outlining how the city plans to raise and spend money each year. The plan projects total 2026 revenues of roughly $873 million, with property taxes making up the largest share at 44.8%, and business properties carrying about 37% of the property tax burden.

briefs.summary: Council is set to receive an information update on improvements to Burnaby's WebReg online recreation registration system, including new pass options, better online access, and changes to how annual passes work.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby's 10 Park Development Projects for 2026–2027
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to receive a report outlining 10 planned park improvement projects for 2026–2027, including playground replacements, a new basketball court, spray park design, and trail upgrades — all funded through the City's 2026–2030 capital plan.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan — Community Feedback Report
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider a 'What We Heard' report summarizing community input on the future of the city's parks, recreation, and culture services, which will shape a new long-term plan called 'Making Life Better' expected to set priorities and service levels going forward.
briefs.action_required: Review the report findings and watch for the next public engagement phase in early 2026 to have your say.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Parks, Recreation & Culture Plan Moves to Drafting Phase
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to receive an update on the city's long-range Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan, which is now entering Phase 3 — the drafting stage. briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if you operate a home care or housekeeping service in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Better at Home housekeeping contract extension end date — Burnaby

briefs.title: Council to Consider: $700,000 Increase to Burnaby Archaeological Consulting Contract
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a pending contract change that would add $700,000 to an existing archaeological consulting services agreement, bringing the total estimated contract value to roughly $2.04 million over three years.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect archaeological consulting work tied to Burnaby development projects.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $700,000 contract extension for archaeological consulting services
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on extending its contract with Campfire Archaeology and Heritage Ltd. by $700,000 to continue archaeological consulting work on City projects. This work helps the City follow BC's Heritage Conservation Act and honour its commitments to Indigenous reconciliation.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if your business does construction or development work in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — archaeological consulting contract change approval

briefs.title: Council to Consider Adding 3 Toyota Tacoma Trucks to City Fleet
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a contract change that would add 3 more Toyota Tacoma pickup trucks to the city fleet for $174,384, using the same pricing as the original 8-truck purchase approved in 2025.

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Buying 3 more Toyota Tacoma trucks for $174,384
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on adding three Toyota Tacoma pickup trucks to the city's Engineering fleet for an estimated $174,384 including taxes. The trucks would replace three aging vehicles, which would then be sold at auction.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $20M fuel supply contract with 4Refuel Canada LP
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a five-year, $20-million contract to 4Refuel Canada LP for the supply and delivery of gasoline, diesel, and biodiesel to City facilities starting March 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on February 10, 2026 — this contract affects fuel supply across City operations.

key_dates.label: Burnaby fuel supply contract with 4Refuel Canada LP — start date
Burnaby fuel supply contract with 4Refuel Canada LP — end date

briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Natural Resources, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to vote on $648,206 contract for new Burnaby garbage truck
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a contract worth $648,206 (including taxes) to Rollins Machinery Ltd. for the purchase of a new tandem axle front load refuse truck — a large commercial garbage truck — to replace an aging city vehicle.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $621,838 contract for two new Burnaby garbage trucks
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a contract worth $621,838 (including taxes) to Rollins Machinery Ltd. for two new split body refuse trucks — specialized vehicles that collect different types of waste in one trip — to replace two aging units currently in service.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Lochdale School Park Playground Replacement Planned for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review site and context maps for a proposed playground replacement at Lochdale School Park, with the upgrade planned for 2026. The maps show the current playground equipment — some dating back to 1989 — and the site's relationship to nearby parks and schools.

key_dates.label: Lochdale School Park playground replacement planned completion year

briefs.title: Council to Consider Permanently Closing Lochdale School Park Junior Playground
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on permanently decommissioning the junior playground at Lochdale School Park and deferring its replacement until future budget cycles can fund it. The playground was closed in December 2025 after failing safety standards, and no replacement is currently budgeted.
briefs.action_required: If you use or operate near Lochdale School Park, watch for the Council decision on February 10, 2026.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Lochdale School Park playground future decision

briefs.title: Council to vote on updates to Burnaby's rules for how advisory bodies are managed
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on proposed changes to the Burnaby Procedure Bylaw that would clarify how Terms of Reference — the rules that define what each advisory body does — are created and updated, requiring the Mayor's direction and a review by the Executive Committee before any changes go to Council.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public notice posted on Burnaby's website and eNewsletter to see the proposed changes before Council votes.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Procedure Bylaw Update — Council consideration meeting date

briefs.title: Council to Consider Restaurant Use at 8340 Fraser Reach Court, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will consider approving a restaurant use at 8340 Fraser Reach Court — a space of about 2,217 sq ft (206 sq m) located near Wiggins St and Riverbend Dr.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if you operate or plan to open a food service business near Fraser Reach Court.

briefs.title: Council to Vote on Removing Restaurant-Only Restriction at Burnaby Industrial Site
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will consider removing a legal restriction (called a covenant) that currently requires a 2,217 sq. ft. space at 8340 Fraser Reach Court to be used as a restaurant only — opening it up to light industrial use instead.
briefs.action_required: If you are interested in the restaurant space at this address, contact the property owner before the restriction is removed.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — covenant discharge vote takes place

briefs.industries: Food Service, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Amendments to Add Five New Zoning Districts
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on sweeping changes to Burnaby's Zoning Bylaw that would create five new zoning districts and update dozens of land-use definitions to support the city's OCP 2050 long-term growth plan. A proposed bylaw would affect rules for industrial, agricultural, institutional, and residential properties across Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed zoning changes to see if your property or business use is affected before Council votes.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Agriculture, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on new zoning rules for industrial, parks, and farm land
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed zoning bylaw that would set out what businesses and uses are allowed — and under what conditions — on lands zoned for employment, industrial, parks, institutional, and agricultural purposes across the city.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property falls under E1, I1, PM1, PM2, or AM1 zoning — new rules could affect what you can operate.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on five new non-residential zoning districts
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on proposed changes to Burnaby's Zoning Bylaw that would create five new zoning categories (areas with specific rules about what can be built or operated there) for non-residential land — covering employment, industrial, agricultural, and other uses — to match the city's long-term land use plan, Burnaby 2050.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business property falls under E1, I1, PM1, PM2, or AM1 zoning — new rules may affect permitted uses.

key_dates.label: Burnaby non-residential zoning bylaw amendments — Council vote

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Vancouver Fraser Port Authority Update on Second Narrows Dredging Works
briefs.summary: The Vancouver Fraser Port Authority is set to appear before Burnaby City Council on February 10, 2026, to give an update on dredging works at Second Narrows — the process of removing sediment from the waterway to keep it navigable for ships.
briefs.action_required: Attend the February 10, 2026 Council meeting if your business is affected by Second Narrows shipping activity.

key_dates.label: Vancouver Fraser Port Authority — Second Narrows dredging update presentation to Burnaby Council

briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Construction & Development, Natural Resources

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approves 2026–2030 Financial Plan and several major contracts
briefs.summary: At its January 27, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on a range of items including a five-year financial plan bylaw, housing agreement changes, and multiple construction and service contracts totalling several million dollars.
briefs.action_required: Review the approved 2026–2030 Financial Plan to understand how city spending may affect your business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Rewrite to Reshape Employment, Industrial & Agricultural Rules
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a major rewrite of its Zoning Bylaw that would cut zoning categories from 53 down to 15 and introduce new rules for employment, industrial, and agricultural lands — including where self-storage facilities can operate and new options for rental housing near SkyTrain stations.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed new zoning districts at the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Rewrite website to see how your business or property may be affected.
